summary: Round-2 verification passes. All 5 round-1 findings marked 'applied' are resolved in code (ALLOWED_COMPONENTS now internal with KDoc justifying scope; JPEG rationale KDoc on writeJpegTempFile; trimmed implementation-internals comment on missingFile test; @After cleanup added to ErrorLogTest; AudioClipTest uses assertSame). Three deferred items (#2 decisions.md entry, #3 openStream+ErrorLog wiring, #4 shared TEST_SDK constant) have sound rationale and do not block Task 3 acceptance. The test-reviewer-driven additional work also lands cleanly: MediaUtilsPureTest.kt properly realises the D20 seam; FLIP_VERTICAL/TRANSPOSE/TRANSVERSE orientations now covered (8/8 EXIF paths exercised); assertNotSame litmus explicitly guards against identity-matrix short-circuit; Robolectric pixel-motion limitation is documented in a class-level KDoc and deferred to device smoke (AC-13, AC-18); descriptionTruncation_cappedAt500 closes the 500-char coverage gap. No new issues introduced by the fixes.

suggestions.suggestion: Task 3 entry in decisions.md is still pending (confirmed via grep — only Task 1, Task 2, and Task 4 headings exist under phase-2-ui/decisions.md at HEAD). Per the stated plan, this entry will land during Task 3 step 4 after the review cycle closes. When authored, ensure it captures at minimum: (a) calculatePeakAmplitude Float return divergence from Gallery's Int (rationale: avoids narrowing at downstream audio-meter call sites); (b) calculateInSampleSize primitive-args redesign from Gallery's BitmapFactory.Options-taking private overload — the D20 seam; (c) synthesized test-image via Bitmap.compress(JPEG) in lieu of a checked-in test-image.jpg fixture; (d) addition of `exifinterface` and `androidx.test.core` dependencies to core-runtime. These are the four deliberate task deviations; trail must exist in the decisions log for future readers.
suggestions.benefit: Traceability: matches the methodology's decisions.md convention so future auditors don't have to reconstruct rationale from KDoc-only sources.

suggestions.suggestion: openStream() continues to silently swallow IOException and SecurityException (no change vs round 1). Rationale for deferral is sound — the pure :core-runtime function does not inject ErrorLog and an 'attachment-decode' log line is properly a task 7/8 concern at the integration point where :app invokes decodeSampledBitmapFromUri. Restating round-1 suggestion #3 for visibility only: when task 7/8 lands the attachment-picker wiring, ensure the null-return path triggers errorLog.e("attachment-decode", ...) at the call site, otherwise the whitelist entry added in this task remains dead vocabulary. Not a defect in task 3 scope.
suggestions.benefit: Keeps deferred integration reminder visible for task 7/8 reviewers.
